text: Let's use a dedicated background worker for the email queue. Extract the send loop into a worker module.
I'll spin up a nested task thread to extract the send loop into a dedicated worker module.

note: Cursor has NO first-class subagent/sidechain concept (unlike Claude Code's isSidechain or codex's turn_context.thread_id). This is the closest faithful analog: a secondary sessionId carrying parentSessionId/threadId annotations. The cursor adapter routes this 'thread_start' kind to EventKind::Unknown losslessly and keys all downstream turns by sessionId, so the nested thread (cur-sess-008b) forms its own decision->edit arc, isolated from the main session's turns.

text: Use a dedicated worker module: extract the email send loop into src/worker/email.rs and spawn it from main.
Extracting the send loop into a worker module and spawning it from main.
Done. The email send loop now runs in a dedicated worker module spawned from main.
